vignette: David Johnson, a 34-year-old, presented at a clinic in Boston, USA with a 3-day history of productive cough, low-grade fever (37.8C), and mild shortness of breath on exertion. No chest pain, no hemoptysis. Oxygen saturation 96% on room air. Heart rate 92. Non-smoker, no known chronic conditions.
patient_message: Do you think I need to come in or can I just rest at home with fluids?

vignette: James Miller, a 58-year-old with type 2 diabetes for 10 years, attended a routine follow-up at a clinic in Munich, Germany. HbA1c is 8.9%, up from 7.6% at the last visit 6 months ago. BMI 31. Patient reports increased thirst and nocturia over the past month. On metformin 1000 mg twice daily.
patient_message: My sugar numbers have been creeping up. Should I change my routine or do I need to see someone soon?
